creator: andreas@naund.org
text:
portupgrade failed on www/rt36

	script() log file with details included, it seems to fail on the dependency
	of mod_perl, however mod_perl2 is installed and it refuses to use it when
	directed to do so via WITH_MODPERL2=yes


Script started on Wed Sep 19 12:00:06 2007
[bsi]/usr/ports/www/rt36# make distclean
===>  Cleaning for mod_perl-1.30
===>  Cleaning for rt-3.6.4
===>  Deleting distfiles for rt-3.6.4
[bsi]/usr/ports/www/rt36# WITH_MODPERL2=yes make
===>  Vulnerability check disabled, database not found

You may use the following build options:

      UPGRADE_RT30                    Upgrading from a RT 3.0 installation
      UPGRADE_RT32                    Upgrading from a RT 3.2 installation
      UPGRADE_RT34                    Upgrading from a RT 3.4 installation
      INITIAL_INSTALL                 Do initialize DB (for initial install only)
      WITH_FASTCGI                    Use FastCGI instead of mod_perl
      WITH_APACHE2                    Use Apache2 as the web server
      MULTIPLE_INSTANCES              Patch RT to allow for multiple instances
                 (see: http://wiki.bestpractical.com/index.cgi?MultipleInstances)

      DB_TYPE=type                    mysql, Oracle, Pg or SQLite (mysql)
      DB_HOST=hostname                The database host (localhost)
      DB_PORT=port                    The database port
      DB_DATABASE=port                The database name (rt3)
      DB_DBA_USER=username            Name of database administrator (root)
      DB_DBA_PASSWORD=password        Password of database administrator
      DB_USER=username                Name of database user for RT (rt_user)
      DB_PASSWORD=password            Name of database password for RT (rt_pass)
